title: epipactis ochroleuca
intro: Epipactis ochroleuca, also known as the pale helleborine, is a species of orchid native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Epipactis ochroleuca include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, while also exhibiting potential antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Epipactis ochroleuca can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be limited due to potential toxicity and the need for further research on its efficacy and safety.
